revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:中右記]]
『'''Chūyūki'''』 (中右記) is a diary written by {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada|ja|藤原宗忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Munetada|1=Fujiwara no Munetada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623159}} from the first year of {{ill|Kanji (era)|en|Kanji (era)|qid=Q1193874|lt=Kanji (era)|1=Kanji (era)}} (1087) to the fourth year of {{ill|Hōen|en|Hōen|qid=Q1193929|lt=Hōen|1=Hōen}} (1138). Although Munetada referred to it as '''Gurin''' (愚林)<ref>Yano Tarō, "Introduction to Chūyūki," p. 1.</ref>, it is commonly called "Chūyūki," an abbreviation of "Diary of the Middle Counselor and Right Minister."<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=Chūyūki Preface |year= |publisher= |author=Toda Yoshimi |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/12239492/1/6 |page=3|qq=}}</ref> == Overview == The diary begins on 11 Year's Day of the fourth year of {{ill|Ōtoku|en|Ōtoku|qid=Q264214|lt=Ōtoku|1=Ōtoku}} (1087), when Munetada was 26 years old. The entries from the first to the fifth year of {{ill|Kanji (era)|en|Kanji (era)|qid=Q1193874|lt=Kanji (era)|1=Kanji (era)}} (1091) were rewritten much later, with Munetada himself revising the entries for the third year (1089), while the other years were edited by his son, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muneyoshi|ja|藤原宗能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Muneyoshi|1=Fujiwara no Muneyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623162}}. As a result, these entries are more concise compared to the later sections, with the third year (1089) being particularly brief. The original manuscripts were discarded after the revisions.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=Chūyūki |publisher= |pages=13–14 |author=Toda Yoshimi |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/12239492/1/11|qq=}}</ref> Despite some missing parts, Chūyūki provides a meticulous record of political events over more than 50 years. It uniquely includes brief biographies akin to those in the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|qid=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} for individuals at the time of their death, an unusual feature for diaries.<ref>Yano Tarō, "Introduction to Chūyūki," p. 5.</ref> It is an invaluable resource for understanding the political, social, and cultural conditions of the early {{ill|Cloistered rule|en|Cloistered rule|qid=Q865416|lt=Insei|1=Cloistered rule}} period. A copy from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} is preserved in the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|qid=Q4346533|lt=Archives and Mausolea Department|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}. == Records of Disasters == Chūyūki documents various disasters observed or reported, providing critical information for estimating their scale and impact: * {{ill|1096|en|1096|qid=Q19485|lt=1096|1=1096}} ({{ill|Kahō|en|Kahō|qid=Q1193280|lt=Kahō|1=Kahō}} 3): Describes damage in Kyoto caused by the {{ill|Eichō earthquake|ja|永長地震|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eichō earthquake|1=1096 Eichō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11550461}} and later mentions destruction in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.<ref>Masahiko Yasuda, *The History of Disaster Recovery in Japan*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, February 1, 2013, p. 16. {{全国書誌番号|22196456|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|1106|en|1106|qid=Q19508|lt=1106|1=1106}} ({{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|qid=Q1193892|lt=Kashō|1=Kajō}} 1): Records the spread of an epidemic.<ref>*The History of Disaster Recovery in Japan*, p. 43.</ref> * {{ill|1108|en|1108|qid=Q19514|lt=1108|1=1108}} ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|qid=Q1193599|lt=Tennin|1=Tennin (era)}} 1), {{ill|October 11|en|October 11|qid=Q2927|lt=October 11|1=October 11}}: Mentions the eruption of {{ill|Mount Asama|en|Mount Asama|qid=Q242730|lt=Mount Asama|1=Mount Asama}} and its aftermath based on accounts from {{ill|Tadanaga|ja|忠長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadanaga|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳川忠長|1=Tokugawa Tadanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1135700}}.<ref>*The History of Disaster Recovery in Japan*, p. 23.</ref> == Publications == * {{ill|Historiographical Institute|en|Historiographical Institute|qid=Q11524667|lt=Tokyo University Historiographical Institute|1=Historiographical Institute}}, ed. *{{ill|Dai Nihon Kokiroku|ja|大日本古記録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Nihon Kokiroku|1=Dai Nihon Kokiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11435645}}: Chūyūki*, Vols. 1–7, Supplement, Iwanami Shoten, 1993–2014 (18 vols. in total). * *Yangming Collection of Records and Documents: Chūyūki*, Shibunkaku Publishing, 1988 (4 vols.). * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Historical Record Collection 8: Chūyūki Vol. 1 (Fujiwara no Munetada) |year=1934 |publisher=Naigai Shoseki |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431536 |editor=Taneo Sasagawa |edition=Tarō Yano|qq=}}. * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Historical Record Collection 9: Chūyūki Vol. 2 (Fujiwara no Munetada) |year=1934 |publisher=Naigai Shoseki |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431537 |editor=Taneo Sasagawa |edition=Tarō Yano|qq=}}. * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Historical Record Collection 10: Chūyūki Vol. 3 (Fujiwara no Munetada) |year=1934 |publisher=Naigai Shoseki |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431538 |editor=Taneo Sasagawa |edition=Tarō Yano|qq=}}. * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Historical Record Collection 11: Chūyūki Vol. 4 (Fujiwara no Munetada) |year=1935 |publisher=Naigai Shoseki |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431539 |editor=Taneo Sasagawa |edition=Tarō Yano|qq=}}. * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Historical Record Collection 12: Chūyūki Vol. 5 (Fujiwara no Munetada) |year=1935 |publisher=Naigai Shoseki |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431540 |editor=Taneo Sasagawa |edition=Tarō Yano|qq=}}. * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Historical Record Collection 13: Chūyūki Vol. 6 (Fujiwara no Munetada) |year=1935 |publisher=Naigai Shoseki |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431541 |editor=Taneo Sasagawa |edition=Tarō Yano|qq=}}. * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Historical Record Collection 14: Chūyūki Vol. 7 (Fujiwara no Munetada) |year=1935 |publisher=Naigai Shoseki |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431542 |editor=Taneo Sasagawa |edition=Tarō Yano|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * Yano Tarō, "Introduction to Chūyūki," in *Expanded Historical Record Collection 9: Chūyūki Vol. 1*, Rinshō Shoin, 1934. * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Chūyūki: The Dynamic People of the Insei Era |year=1979 |publisher=Société |author=Toda Yoshimi |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/12239492|qq=}}. * Matsuzono Hitoshi, "The Formation of the Fujiwara no Munetada's Family Record," in *Records of the Household: The Documentation of the Medieval State*, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1997. * Sugimoto Osamu, "The Obituary Narratives in Chūyūki," *Ancient Culture* 45-1, 1993. * Sato Kenji, "Chūyūki (Fujiwara no Munetada): Shirakawa Insei through Munetada's Eyes," in *Reading Medieval Japanese History through Diaries*, Minerva Shobo, 2011. * Matsuzono Hitoshi, "A Man Who Fulfilled His Life: Fujiwara no Munetada's Chūyūki," in *Those Enchanted by Diaries*, Rinshō Shoin, 2017. {{Lit-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|中右記|version=103805268|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:中右記]]
